title: Arachnorchis phaeoclavia
taxonomy: Arachnorchis phaeoclavia is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid native to Western Australia. The classification of this orchid includes its genus Arachnorchis, which is often divided into subgenera, and its specific epithet phaeoclavia. The Arachnorchis genus is also sometimes placed within the subtribe Megastigmatinae. This classification helps to identify and organize the unique characteristics of Arachnorchis phaeoclavia within the larger orchid family.

intro: Arachnorchis phaeoclavia, commonly known as the Grey Spider Orchid, is a rare and endemic species of orchid found in the southwestern region of Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Arachnorchis phaeoclavia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Arachnorchis phaeoclavia is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or ointment to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
